Tests the behavior of the `Number` function with a non-numeric string input.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the type of the result when the `Number` function is called with a non-numeric string argument, `"abcdefg"`. The `assert` statement verifies that the type of the result is a `number`, which is consistent with JavaScript's behavior of converting non-numeric strings to the special numeric value `NaN` (Not-a-Number), which is still of type `number`. This code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's type conversion behavior.
